title: centaurea stellata
intro: Centaurea stellata, also known as the Jay's honeysuckle or mountain bluet, is a flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea stellata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Centaurea stellata can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve to apply topically.
